rule_text: Tenders by public advertisement in AP may NOT be restricted to firms on the approved contractor list; firms NOT on the list must be informed they are at liberty to tender on payment of prescribed fee; if their tender is satisfactory, the firm's capability is to be verified before order placement, and on satisfactory execution, the firm is added to the approved list.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule III Instruction (14)
verification_method: Check NIT does NOT exclude non-approved-list firms from tendering; new-bidder capability-verification process documented

rule_text: AP Government financial patronage (contracts, supply orders, services, permits, licences, quotas, priorities) shall not be granted unless the bidder produces an INCOME-TAX VERIFICATION CERTIFICATE in the prescribed form from the Income-tax Officer of the assessment circle/ward/district.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule III Instruction (14) Note
verification_method: Check bidder eligibility file contains current ITCC/PAN+income-tax-verification document

rule_text: AP stores shall NOT be paid for until the receiving officer has taken delivery, checked quantity AND quality, AND found them satisfactory in every respect; partial advance against railway-receipt is permissible ONLY for distant suppliers of well-known standing AND only after pre-agreement secures Government against loss for short/defective supply.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule III Instruction (16)
verification_method: Check tender SCC payment-after-delivery-and-inspection clause; advance-against-RR allowed only with reputed-firm + pre-agreement

rule_text: Cash deposits as EARNEST MONEY must as a rule be demanded from ALL tenderers in AP stores procurement — even from firms of established repute; the deposit may be DISPENSED WITH only by general/special orders of the Heads of Departments concerned.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule III Instruction (17) — Memo No 33621/423/Accts/70-14 dated 15-10-1971
verification_method: Check EMD on file for every bidder; if waiver applied, verify HOD's general/special exemption order

rule_text: EMD from intending tenderers in OTHER STATES (non-AP) shall be made by Crossed Bank Drafts from Scheduled Banks or State Bank of India; the proceeds must be remitted into the treasury by the departmental officer in the name of the parties concerned.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule III Instruction (17) — GO Ms No 42 / Finance dated 4-2-1976
condition_when: TenderState=AndhraPradesh AND TenderType=Goods AND BidderState != AndhraPradesh
verification_method: Out-of-state-bidder EMD must be Crossed BD from Scheduled Bank/SBI; treasury-remittance record on file

rule_text: All AP stores articles (whether Indian or foreign-manufactured) are subject to INSPECTION before acceptance; articles for which specifications/tests have been prescribed by competent authority must conform to such specs and satisfy prescribed tests, which may be carried out during manufacture, before, or after despatch.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule IV
verification_method: Check tender SCC contains pre-acceptance inspection clause + IS/spec/test conformance requirement

rule_text: Important plant, machinery, and iron-and-steel work for AP shall be obtained ONLY from firms approved by the Chief Controller of Stores, Indian Stores Department, New Delhi (specified in lists issued from time to time); tenders from non-approved firms are not summarily rejected but referred to CCS for capability assessment.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule VI
condition_when: TenderState=AndhraPradesh AND TenderType=Goods AND Material IN [Plant, Machinery, IronSteelStructural]
verification_method: Check tender QR requires CCS-approved-firms list filtering for plant/machinery/iron-steel; or reference to CCS approval for non-listed bidder

rule_text: Inter-departmental purchases between AP Government departments are permitted under Article 125 Rule VII; purchasing officers must give 5% price-preference to articles available from Government institutions (Jails, Forest Department, PWD Workshops, Industrial Schools, Government industries) over open-market sources.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule VII Instructions (1)-(2)
verification_method: Check inter-departmental supply request precedes private-firm tender; if private-firm awarded, verify Govt-institution-quoted-price > Govt-institution-price + 5%

rule_text: AP standardised office furniture must be procured INVARIABLY from the Jail Department; orders sent at start of official year; disputes over standard-conformance resolved by 3-member committee (consuming dept rep + Jail Department rep + technical-knowledge expert from Industries Dept).
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule VII Instruction 2(a)
verification_method: Office-furniture tenders must show Jail-Department-cannot-supply certificate before private-firm sourcing

rule_text: Before placing AP stores orders with private firms, the surplus stock of any usable articles in OTHER GOVERNMENT DEPARTMENTS must first be utilised, irrespective of cost; Heads of Departments must circulate surplus-stores lists; tender proposals must state whether surplus utilisation was checked.
source_clause: AP Financial Code Vol-I, Article 125 Rule VII Instruction (3)
verification_method: Check tender file contains surplus-stores-utilisation-checked declaration; verify cross-departmental enquiry record
